groups.context_description: Life as contingent on inner-being orientation — obedience, fear, seeking, and choosing God as the condition of life; also surviving divine encounter
Word of God and the Spirit as source of inner-being life — life received from God's word, promise, or Spirit as sustaining reality of the inner person
Divine revival of the inner person — God restoring life to the spirit and heart of those depleted, contrite, or despairing
Inner-being wrestling with life — weight, value, perplexity, and hope of being alive in the face of death
God's gift of reviving — inner-being renewal of a people or person after affliction, understood as divine favour overturning desolation
Living God (Aramaic) as object of inner-being acknowledgment, worship, and trust — enduring reality as ground for inner person's orientation
The living as those with capacity for knowledge of God and intercession — inner-being capacities distinctive to those alive
Living in inner-being suspension — physically alive but existentially and relationally confined, as if dead
Living God as ground and object of the inner person's deepest orientation — soul's thirst, worship, and trust directed to the living God
Life as the God-given condition within which the inner person exists, fears, and responds — fearing God all one's days
Inner-being quality of the living — awareness of mortality, hope, wisdom, and thanksgiving as distinguishing capacities of those alive
Life as fruit of divine redemption and inner-being turning — restoration to life as outcome of repentance, trust, or divine care
Term names divine favour — God's inner disposition of acceptance, goodwill, and relational warmth extended to a person or people
Term names what is acceptable or delightful to God — God's inner moral and aesthetic standard by which offerings, conduct, and persons are accepted or rejected
Term names the divine will as sovereign purpose — what God wills and takes pleasure in as an act of inner purposive authority
Term names human will or pleasure — what the person wills, desires, or takes pleasure in as an expression of their inner authority or appetite
Term names the quality of desirability in an object — what makes it a focus of inner longing, lust, or delight; the magnetic draw the object exerts on the inner being
Term names delight as the orientation of the inner being toward its object — the inner disposition of willing, delighting in, or taking pleasure in what one loves
Term names the cherished object of deepest desire — what the inner being holds most dear, and whose loss or destruction evokes profound grief
Term names desirability or preciousness as the quality that draws the inner being — what is cherished, longed for, or grieved when lost
Term names destructive inner desire — the evil craving or ruinous orientation of the wicked person's soul, which drives harm and captures the person from within
Term names the object of inner desire — what the person specifically longs for or purposes; desire as directed toward a concrete end
Term names the inner longing or attachment of a person — the soul's desire for another person, or purposive desire directed toward what one wills to accomplish
Term names divine inner attachment — God's love set on a person or people as an act of inner binding
Term names the destructive craving of the wicked — desire as an inner force that boasts, grasps, goes unsatisfied, and ultimately brings ruin
Term names the righteous person's desire — the inner longing that orients toward God or what is genuinely good, and that God hears and fulfils
Term names desire as a primal inner state — the original experience of the inner being drawn toward what appears good or what the appetite craves
Term names desire as a deep relational force of attraction — the inner pull of one being toward another, capable of being redemptive (Song), covenantal (Gen 3), or menacing (Gen 4)
Term names lust or erotic desire as an inner disposition — the inner appetite that shapes speech and outward conduct while the heart remains elsewhere
Term names the concentrated inner wish — desire expressed as an exclamation of intense longing or strong wish
Term names the desires of the heart as petitions before God — what the inner being asks for and hopes to receive
Term names divine pleasure, purpose, or delight — what God takes pleasure in, purposes, or wills
Term names human delight or pleasure as inner orientation — what the person takes pleasure in, delights in, or values most
Term names desire as practical want or request — what someone asks for or desires in a relational or practical context
Term names the absence of desire or care — the inner state of indifference, being unmourned, or valued by no one
Term names prayer as urgent petition to God — the inner movement of the person toward God in need, crisis, or desire for understanding
Term names petition directed toward human authority — the inner orientation of request toward one in power
Term names inquiring of God — the inner orientation of seeking divine guidance, oracle, or provision through direct petition or prayer
Term names asking or inquiring of a person — relational request, petition, or inquiry directed to another human
Term names inquiry by divination — seeking oracles through occult or disordered means; the inner orientation misdirected toward forbidden spiritual channels
Term names God questioning or requiring — God's inner purposive demand or questioning addressed to humans
Term names asking as the expression of personal desire or inner appetite — the person's own craving or longing framed as a request
Term names God's elective choosing of a person or people — the expression of divine inner will in covenant election and appointment
Term names God's choosing of a place — divine designation of a location as the dwelling of his name and the site of covenant worship
Term names the human moral or volitional choosing — the inner will of the person directed toward good or evil, wisdom or folly, God or idols
Term names practical human choosing — selecting persons or options in leadership, strategy, or administration; the act of directed human preference
Term names craving or appetite as a disruptive inner force — desire for physical gratification that overrides proper orientation
Term names desire as the governing orientation of the soul — what the inner being inclines toward, craves, or chooses as its object
Term names intense personal longing — the soul expressing deep yearning toward a specific person or object
Term names the sovereign desire of God — his inner purposive longing or choice expressed as divine will toward its object
Term names coveting — the prohibited inner desire for what belongs to another; desire as acquisitive grasping that violates boundaries
Term names desire as positive inner orientation — delight in what is genuinely good, beautiful, or worthy
Term names idolatrous or misdirected desire — inner longing directed toward what does not profit or toward spiritual falsehood
Term names the divine inner desire — God's own longing for a place or his people
Term names the directing or lifting of the inner being toward its object — lifting the soul to God in trust and prayer, or the inner orientation turned toward falsehood, gain, or longing
Term names intense personal longing — the inner being straining, fainting, or yearning toward what it desires with great force
Term names intense panting desire — the inner being straining urgently, gasping, or panting toward its object; longing expressed as bodily urgency
Term names seeking God — the inner orientation of the person directed toward God in prayer, inquiry, worship, repentance, or longing for his presence
Term names the hostile will seeking to harm or destroy — the inner intention of enmity expressed as purposive pursuit of another's life
Term names seeking wisdom, understanding, or moral good — the inner orientation of the mind toward truth, knowledge, or virtue
Term names seeking the beloved — the inner longing of the person for another in love, relational desire, or longing for comfort
Term names petitioning or seeking a person in authority — the inner orientation of dependence, request, or enquiry directed toward a human or spiritual power
Term names God seeking — the divine inner purposive search for righteousness, the lost, or a faithful person; and divine scrutiny of the human inner being
Term names human compassion or its withholding — the inner disposition of pity or mercy toward another, and its refusal
Term names divine compassion or its withholding — God's inner disposition of pity or mercy toward his people, and its withdrawal in judgment
Term names the sovereign divine will — God's inner purposive willing expressed in the giving, ordering, and overturning of kingdoms
Term names the human inner desire for understanding — the person's will directed toward knowing truth
The desires of the flesh — competing inner orientation against God, driving sin
Earnest desire as positive inner-being orientation — longing for what is good
Desire as the generative mechanism — origin of sin in the inner being
Divine goodwill/pleasure as the sovereign inner purpose of God
Human goodwill/desire — the inner orientation of longing for good
Long-held longing for relational presence
Longing as inner-being response to godly grief and separation
Term names consuming inner appetite or passion driving disordered conduct — desire as an overpowering inner force
The one who desires evil — inner orientation defined by craving what is wrong
Term names a determined purposive will — human deliberate intention or divine sovereign resolve — as the inner origin of directed action
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Pleasing as the inner orientation aligned with what God approves
Asking Jesus — the inner-being posture of those who approach Jesus with need or faith
Jesus asking the Father — his inner-being orientation of prayer and intercession
Apostolic asking — pastoral concern expressed as appeal
Forceful demanding desire — the aggressive inner claim of hostile acquisition
Term names a compelling inner passion or sensual desire that draws the person away from a prior commitment — the force of inner appetite overriding prior orientation
Term names deep personal affective longing — an inner state of tender desire for another person
Longing for persons — deep inner-being desire for relational presence
Longing for God, the heavenly, or spiritual nourishment
Desire as covetous or lustful inner orientation
Deep earnest longing as a positive inner-being orientation
Term names the inner act of human willing, intending, or desiring — the volitional faculty in motion toward an object or action
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names God's or Christ's inner purposive will — sovereign divine willing that directs revelation, creation, and redemption
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names the comfort directed toward the afflicted inner being — the act of comforting another in suffering, grief, or distress, and the inner state of being comforted
Term names the urgent plea or earnest appeal arising from inner need, urgency, or desperation — the inner state of the petitioner expressed outward
Term names the pastoral or apostolic appeal — the inner relational care and authority of the one urging another toward faithful conduct and inner transformation
Term names the inner orientation of the will and desire directed toward God, his kingdom, or righteousness — the commanded posture of the disciple
Term exposes the inward misdirection of desire toward self-interest, self-glory, or human approval in contrast to God-oriented orientation
Term names the inner movement of the person toward God himself — in longing, worship, encounter, or relational desire
Term names the inner demand or desire for external confirmation — seeking a sign, proof, or wisdom as a substitute for trust
Term names the inward intention of hostility, betrayal, or lethal opposition expressed through purposeful directed action
Term names the paradox of inner self-preservation instinct and its inversion — the desire to preserve life as the condition of losing it, or to seek death when life is unbearable
Seeking after earthly or invalid things — misdirected inner orientation
Seeking what is truly good — inner-being orientation toward the word and the heavenly
Term names the divided inner will — the person wills the good but finds themselves doing what they do not will; inner conflict between desire and action
Term names God's own inner willing — his purposive desire toward mercy, election, redemption, and his sovereign ordering of all things
Term names the submitted or aligned will — human willingness to follow, yield, or conform to God's will, including the servant-pattern of self-denial
Term names the misdirected or self-serving will — human willing oriented toward wrong ends, defiance, self-advancement, or harm
Term names the direction of the human will in ordinary personal, relational, and pastoral contexts — what the person inclines toward, requests, prefers, or intends
Term names the divine will as the object of human alignment, obedience, and discernment — the inner posture of submission to God's will
Term names the divine will as God's sovereign inner purpose — the ground of election, calling, providence, and redemption
Term names the human will or desire as an inner faculty — capable of self-determination, misdirection, or capture by an opposing power
Term names the inner state of security or settled ease — the condition of feeling safe and undisturbed, even when groundless
Term names the inner act of looking to God — the orientation of the person's gaze toward God in prayer, trust, hope, or longing
Term names looking that discloses an inner state — contempt, envy, mourning, longing, or divine regard withheld — where the looking reveals an inner disposition
Term names the divine act of looking — God's inner attentive regard toward humanity, which is relational inner engagement
Term names the inner act of hoping in God — the orientation of the soul toward God's steadfast love and salvation as the ground of sustained expectation
Term names hope tested or disappointed — waiting in vain, hoping for good only to receive evil, or hoping despite anguish and slaying
Term names the inner act of patient waiting — the posture of sustained expectant attention, whether directed toward God, toward counsel, or through suffering toward renewal
Term names confidence as an inner quality — the settled inner assurance grounded in fear of God and integrity of conduct
Term names the inner condition of cognitive and moral dullness — the stupidity that results from following false instruction and worshipping worthless things
Term names the refuge or shelter sought — the object toward which the inner act of seeking safety is directed, here misplaced and turned to shame
Term names hope as the inner orientation of a people toward God — the expectation of restoration and faithfulness grounded in God as the source of communal hope
Term names confidence in God — the inner quality of assurance and reliance directed toward God as the ground of blessing and security
Term names confidence in inadequate objects — trust placed in gold, human strongholds, or false gods, which is severed or leads to shame
Term names hope as the fundamental inner expectation of the person — the capacity for anticipating good, future, and restoration, present or lost, fulfilled or cut off
Term names expectation as an inner orientation — the forward-looking hope directed at a specific source, here misplaced and turned to shame at its collapse
Term names hope as an inner quality — the expectation of the person that produces joy when fulfilled and heart-sickness when deferred
Term names the perishing or collapse of hope — inner hope withdrawn, lost, or proven false
Term names hope in God — the inner orientation of trust and expectation toward God as the source of blessing and protection
Term names God as the inner person's refuge — the place of safety and strength to which the whole person turns in distress, fear, and trouble
Term names a misplaced inner refuge — false security found in lies or inadequate protection, which God will sweep away
Term names the inner act of waiting for God — the sustained inner orientation of expectation directed toward God's salvation, help, and promised action
Term names the inner experience of unfulfilled or hostile waiting — hope directed toward good but met with evil, or enemies' anticipation
Term names the inner act of looking to or hoping in God — the expectant orientation of persons or creation toward God as provider and savior
Term names inner despair or hopelessness — the giving up of hope or expectation, whether as a state, a declaration, or the inner capitulation of pursuit
Term names the inner act of taking refuge in God — the whole-person orientation of fleeing to God as the source of protection, safety, and deliverance
Term names the inner act of taking refuge in inadequate objects — misplaced refuge in Egypt, human power, or idols, contrasted with refuge in God
Term names the inner act of trusting in God — the orientation of the whole person's reliance and confidence directed toward God as the ground of security
Term names trust in persons, things, or self — misplaced or tested inner reliance on inadequate objects, contrasted with trust in God
Term names trust as an inner state of security or ease — the condition of being settled, unguarded, or confident in one's circumstances
Term names the ground or object of inner trust — what the person's confidence rests on, whether God or human power
Term names the inner act of waiting quietly — a posture of patient, still expectation directed toward God's salvation
Term names the inner orientation of wish or longing — the expression of a strong desire for something not presently the case, disclosing an inward disposition toward a desired reality
Term names hope as an eschatological inner orientation — the confident forward expectation toward God's promised future, grounding present endurance and joy
Term names hope as a constitutive permanent inner quality held alongside faith and love — one of the abiding characteristics of the inner person
Term names the absence or loss of hope as an inner condition of desolation — the state of the person without God or without future
Term names the inner disposition of expecting nothing in return — the absence of self-directed hope as the posture of genuine love
Term names the inner act of being first to hope in Christ — the prior orientation of hope toward him before his revelation became widespread
Term names the inner act of setting hope on God or Christ — the orientation of the whole person's expectation toward God as the source of salvation and the ground of trust
Term names the inner act of hoping for a specific future outcome, person, or event — forward orientation of expectation toward something not yet present
The beloved person as object of deep inner longing — toward whom the heart reaches in yearning affection
Term marks completion of an inner act — prayer or inward speech — naming the moment the inner address to God concludes
Term names the ending or exhaustion of an inner state or divine attribute — spent quality or inexhaustible character
Consuming action of God's inner wrath, anger, or jealousy — divine inner passion expressed in judgment
Consuming as consequence of moral/spiritual failure — person or community consumed by inner rebellion or hardness
Spent or failing inner self — strength, heart, life, or hope exhausted through grief, longing, or affliction
Spending or exhausting of God's inner wrath, anger, or fury in judgment — divine inner passion expressed to its limit
Full-end capacity of divine judgment and its deliberate restraint — expressions of God's inner moral character, whether wrath or mercy
Inner resolution or fixed determination — settled inner purpose that drives subsequent action
Soul's consuming longing — inner being spent in yearning for God, his salvation, and his word
Extreme or total quality of an inner state — unsearchable limit of God; completeness of inner moral orientation
Failing of inner faculties — trembling heart, failing eyes, languishing soul — marks of the person under judgment
Limit of human perfection perceived by the discerning inner mind — set against the inexhaustible scope of God's commandment
Failing of the eyes through consuming longing — inner grief of powerless yearning for what has been taken
Rushing, yearning intensity of the soul's inner longing — deep craving driving the inner being toward what it desires
All-consuming longing of the soul for God's word and rules — inner being entirely taken up with desire for the divine
Intense, forward-straining inner disposition of eager expectation — longing that stretches the whole being toward a not-yet-revealed future
Term names defiling passion as an inner drive — the desire that defiles the person who yields to it
Term names the inner posture of patient attentiveness — the disposition of measured, unhurried receptiveness toward another's speech
Term names moral or ritual defilement — the corruption or staining of the inner person (mind, conscience) or the body as expressive of inner moral disorder
Term names the deepest inner yearning of the soul — intense longing for the sanctuary as cherished object
Term names the sick/morbid craving of the inner person — the unhealthy inner obsession with controversy that produces envy, dissension, and evil suspicions
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the inner aspiration toward the heavenly — the deep desire that orients the inner person beyond the earthly toward what God has prepared
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names illness as the condition that Jesus heals — the embodied suffering that he takes upon himself and from which he liberates, fulfilling the Servant's vicarious identification with human weakness
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored
